# Disclaimer

## 1. Important Notice

**CAP-CDSS is a research prototype developed for the MedGemma Impact Challenge. It is not a medical device. All clinical outputs are generated by artificial intelligence and must not be used for patient care decisions.**

---

## 2. Regulatory Status

This software:

- Is **not** cleared or approved by the U.S. Food and Drug Administration (FDA)
- Is **not** CE-marked under the EU Medical Device Regulation (MDR 2017/745)
- Is **not** registered with the UK Medicines and Healthcare products Regulatory Agency (MHRA)
- Does **not** hold classification as a Class I, II, or III medical device under any jurisdiction
- Has **not** undergone clinical validation with real patient data
- Is **not** registered with any health technology assessment body

No regulatory submission has been made or is pending in any jurisdiction.

---

## 3. AI-Generated Content

All clinical outputs produced by this system — including but not limited to severity scores, CURB65 calculations, antibiotic recommendations, contradiction alerts, chest X-ray (CXR) analysis, lab interpretation, monitoring plans, and clinician summaries — are generated by MedGemma 1.5 4B (`google/medgemma-1.5-4b-it`), an artificial intelligence model.

AI-generated outputs:

- **May contain errors, omissions, or hallucinations**
- **May produce clinically inappropriate or dangerous recommendations**
- **Have been validated only on synthetic (simulated) cases**, not real patient data
- **Do not reflect the judgement of a qualified healthcare professional**
- **Must not be relied upon** for any clinical decision

The deterministic clinical logic components (CURB65 scoring, antibiotic selection rules, discharge criteria, stewardship rules) are implemented in Python based on published evidence, but their integration with AI-extracted data means the end-to-end system output is not guaranteed to be correct.

---

## 4. No Medical Advice

Nothing in this software, its documentation, notebooks, or outputs constitutes medical advice, diagnosis, or treatment recommendation. All content is provided for research, educational, and demonstration purposes only.

All clinical decisions must be made by qualified, licensed healthcare professionals exercising independent clinical judgement in accordance with applicable standards of care, local guidelines, and individual patient circumstances.

This system is designed to **support**, not **replace**, clinical judgement.

---

NO WARRANTY IS MADE REGARDING THE CLINICAL ACCURACY, SAFETY, OR RELIABILITY OF ANY OUTPUT PRODUCED BY THIS SYSTEM.

## 8. Data Privacy

### Chest X-Ray Images

This repository includes 12 de-identified chest X-ray images sourced from the [NIH Clinical Center ChestX-ray8 dataset](https://nihcc.app.box.com/v/ChestXray-NIHCC), a publicly available dataset released with no usage restrictions. These images were annotated and published as part of the [RSNA Pneumonia Detection Challenge (2018)](https://www.rsna.org/rsnai/ai-image-challenge/rsna-pneumonia-detection-challenge-2018). Attribution: Wang et al. (IEEE CVPR, 2017); Shih et al. (Radiology: AI, 2019).

These images are real, de-identified medical images. They contain no personally identifiable information and are distributed under public domain terms by the NIH Clinical Center.

### Clinical Scenarios

All clinical scenarios in this repository — including patient names, demographics, FHIR bundles, lab reports, clinical notes, and vital signs — are **entirely synthetic** (simulated). No real patient clinical records were used in the development, training, or testing of this system.

### Deployment Responsibility

Any party deploying or adapting this system for use with real patient data assumes full responsibility for compliance with all applicable data protection regulations, including but not limited to:

- UK General Data Protection Regulation (UK GDPR) and Data Protection Act 2018
- EU General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR)
- U.S. Health Insurance Portability and Accountability Act (HIPAA)
- Any other applicable local, national, or international data protection laws

---

## 9. Intended Use

This software was developed as a research prototype for the [MedGemma Impact Challenge](https://www.kaggle.com/competitions/medgemma-impact-challenge) (Kaggle, 2026).

**Intended uses:**

- Research demonstration of agentic AI workflows in clinical decision support
- Educational exploration of cross-modal contradiction detection
- Benchmarking MedGemma's capabilities on structured clinical tasks

**NOT intended for:**

- Clinical decision-making
- Patient care or treatment planning
- Diagnostic purposes
- Any use in a real clinical, hospital, or healthcare setting
- Any use where the output may influence decisions about real patients
